title: Alyssum alpestre lusus microphyllum
taxonomy: Alyssum alpestre lusus microphyllum is a botanical name classified under the family Brassicaceae. This classification places it within the order Brassicales. Its classification highlights its botanical significance and relationship to other Brassicaceae family members.

intro: Alyssum alpestre lusus microphyllum, also known as mountain alyssum, is a small, annual or biennial plant belonging to the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its tiny, white flowers and delicate foliage. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of mountain alyssum include isothiocyanates, glucosinolates, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from mountain alyssum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions, like eczema and acne.
